acceptance_targets: [ch03-booker-001] Pass if a reader can move from the revised ending of chapter_03 directly into the boundary pack's chapter_04 opening without any temporal rewind. Fail if the revised chapter still shows Beni having already completed the Mayfair run, picked up Bea, or returned to the kitchen after the day's work is done.

[ch03-craft-001] Pass if the revised passage no longer resolves the whole afternoon in a single summary paragraph. The page must either omit that material from chapter_03 or dramatize one selected errand so a reader can point to at least one concrete action, one spoken exchange, and one changed power relation in the revised span.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can move from the revised ending of chapter_03 directly into the boundary pack's chapter_04 opening without any temporal rewind. Fail if the revised chapter still shows Beni having already completed the Mayfair run, picked up Bea, or returned to the kitchen after the day's work is done.
findings.problem: The chapter overruns its boundary and breaks causal handoff. After the noon setup, it jumps ahead to a completed Mayfair errand, Bea pickup, and an end-of-day return to the kitchen, so chapter_03 finishes after material that the boundary context reserves for the next chapter. That creates timeline backtracking and weakens the chapter's own ending because the reader has already been carried past the handoff point.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or relocate the late-afternoon/evening material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:367, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:397, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:405 so chapter_03 ends before the post-noon Nina run is completed. A clean option is to land the chapter on the first noon-to-afternoon pull of attachment around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:353-365, then let chapter_04 own the Mayfair trip and everything after it.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage no longer resolves the whole afternoon in a single summary paragraph. The page must either omit that material from chapter_03 or dramatize one selected errand so a reader can point to at least one concrete action, one spoken exchange, and one changed power relation in the revised span.
findings.problem: The line 'The afternoon flattened into the kind of service work that left no mark except tiredness' is sanitizing summary: it replaces a plot-bearing afternoon with generic compression right where the chapter needs either a clean stop or concrete scene pressure. Because the style rules explicitly disallow summary replacing scene, this reads as timidity and craft evasion rather than purposeful ellipsis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:367-395 so the chapter does one thing clearly. Either delete this afternoon summary entirely and end before it, or choose one specific afternoon beat in that span and render it in scene with concrete physical detail, dialogue, and a visible leverage shift. Do not keep the current one-paragraph synopsis of Nina's meeting, garment bags, and Bea pickup.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into asymmetry, embarrassment, appetite, and the material costs of intimacy. When an edge case appears, choose the version that preserves class pressure, erotic consequence, and bodily specificity rather than smoothing the scene into tasteful distance or soft-focus safety.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene naturally contains sexual awkwardness, bodily shame, class contempt, grief, or moral compromise, vague tasteful phrasing counts as a failed draft and must be rewritten toward concrete consequence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms should appear mainly for precision, social performance, or emphasis.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary chapter feel through scene count, dialogue density, movement versus waiting, and whether the turn comes from confrontation, discovery, aftermath, preparation, or unwanted intrusion. A nocturnal chauffeur chapter should not be followed by another chapter with the same pulse and architecture; counter hard chapters with domestic or reflective ones that still advance consequence.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken but exact, materially grounded, and alert to social register; imagery should emerge from labor, appetite, weather, and bodily memory rather than from decorative abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person anchored to Beni, with memory pressure and sensory bleed but no roaming omniscience.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate compressed, road-tight paragraphs with slower observational passages that let smell, touch, and silence accumulate; keep sentence music controlled rather than showy.
